name: Old -Time Taffy Pull
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, combine sugar, corn syrup, water, butter, cornstarch, and salt. Cook until the candy thermometer reads 256°F (or until a small amount forms a hard ball when dropped into cold water), about 20 minutes.
3. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla, almond extract, almonds, and food coloring. Pour into the pan. Let cool to a lukewarm texture in the center and at the edges for 15-20 minutes.
4. Have guests fold, double, and pull the taffy until it is light in color and stiff. If the taffy sticks, have them lightly butter their hands.
5. Cut into pieces with scissors. Guests can help wrap each piece in plastic wrap (taffy will lose its shape if not wrapped).
6. Makes about 1 pound of taffy.
If the taffy becomes too stiff to pull, reheat it in a 350°F oven for 3-5 minutes. Alternatively, omit almond extract, almonds, and food coloring, stir in vanilla and 1 package (6 ounces) semisweet chocolate or butter-scotch chips after removing from heat, let cool, then cut into 1-inch squares. Shape the candy into rolls, decorate with nuts, or wrap the squares around miniature marshmallows.
